title: Understanding the Problem
text: The block is on a smooth ramp, which means there is no friction. The only forces acting on the block are gravity and the normal force from the ramp.

title: Components of Gravity
text: The gravitational force acting on the block can be split into two components: one parallel to the ramp and one perpendicular to the ramp.

title: Perpendicular Component of Gravity
text: The component of gravity perpendicular to the ramp is what the normal force balances. This component can be calculated using the formula: $$m g cos\left(\theta\right)$$.

title: Calculate the Perpendicular Component
text: Use the formula $$m g cos\left(\theta\right)$$ to find the perpendicular component of gravity. Given: $$m=2.0$$ kg, $$g=9.8$$ m/s², $$theta=30$$ degrees.
